title: Kobe Bryant card collection inventory
html:
TL;DR

  • Single-line Sept-1-2020 inventory of seven Kobe Bryant trading cards Yahir owned: 1997 Hoops #75 + #15/30, 1998 Upper Deck #58, 2007 Topps #24, 2008/09 Fleer Hot Pros #13, 2009 Bowman #24, 2009/10 Upper Deck #MA-KB and #NA1.
  • Captured nine months after Kobe's January-2020 death — Yahir was actively collecting/cataloging Kobe cards in the immediate post-death price-spike era.
  • Pairs with the Sept 23 sports cards mentor note three weeks later — Yahir was workshopping a sports cards business thesis in the same fall as senior-year COVID returned.
  • The rookie/early-career era weighting (1997, 1998 cards) suggests Yahir was specifically pursuing Kobe's scarcity tier, not just any year — early collector instinct showing a value-investing lens.
  • First Kobe mention in vault as a collected entity, distinct from later mentions as a mamba-mentality reference; the physical-card version of Yahir's parasocial relationship to Kobe predates the idea version.

Note

A one-line inventory. Yahir at 17 logging seven Kobe cards by year, set, and number — the sports cards as investment frame surfacing as proper accounting (the #15/30 notation is serial-number tracking, the kind of detail a collector-as-investor would care about). Three weeks later he'd be drafting questions for a sports-cards mentor (2020 09 23 sports cards mentor questions); two months earlier he was building COVID stock watchlists. The throughline is Yahir at 17 trying multiple flavors of investment exposure — equities, alt-protein, sports cards — before committing to any one. The Kobe-specific focus also reads as parasocial: Kobe died Jan-26-2020, and by September Yahir is meaningfully invested.

Connections

  • kobe bryant — collected-entity first mention
  • trading cards as business
  • 2020 09 23 sports cards mentor questions — three-weeks-later companion
  • 2020 03 22 stock watchlist march — same-fall investment-experimentation
  • · grants pass teen era
